title: Understanding the Problem
text: To solve this problem, we need to determine how many turns the grindstone makes before it stops. We will use the work-energy theorem, which relates the work done by friction to the change in rotational kinetic energy.

title: Calculate the Moment of Inertia
text: The moment of inertia (I) for a uniform cylindrical grindstone is given by the formula $$I=\frac{1}{2} M R^2$$. Use the given mass $$(M=10$$ kg) and radius $$(R=0.12$$ m) to calculate I.

title: Moment of Inertia Calculation
text: Calculate the moment of inertia using the formula $$I=\frac{1}{2} M R^2$$.

title: Determine the Frictional Torque
text: The frictional force $$F_{friction}$$ is given by $$F_{friction}=coefficient_{of,friction} normal_{force}$$. The torque (τ) due to this frictional force is $$τ=F_{friction} R$$.

title: Frictional Torque Calculation
text: Calculate the frictional torque using $$τ=F_{friction} R$$, where $$F_{friction}=0.8\times5$$ N and $$R=0.12$$ $$m$$.

title: Apply the Work-Energy Theorem
text: The work done by the frictional torque is equal to the change in rotational kinetic energy. Use the formula: $$work=\tau \theta$$, where θ is the angular displacement in radians.

title: Calculate Angular Displacement
text: Use the work-energy theorem to find θ: $$work=\tau \theta$$. The initial kinetic energy is $$\frac{1}{2} I {\omega}^2$$, where ω is the initial angular velocity.
